questions.question: What is the primary characteristic that distinguishes a Harvard architecture from a Von Neumann architecture?
options: Harvard architecture uses separate memory spaces for instructions and data, while Von Neumann uses a single shared memory space.
Von Neumann architecture supports pipelining, while Harvard architecture does not.
Harvard architecture is exclusively used in general-purpose CPUs, while Von Neumann is for embedded systems.
Von Neumann architecture has a larger instruction set compared to Harvard architecture.
Harvard architecture utilizes a write-through cache policy, whereas Von Neumann uses write-back.

questions.question: In low-power digital IC design, what is the primary purpose of employing "clock gating" techniques?
options: To reduce dynamic power consumption by selectively disabling the clock signal to idle functional blocks.
To provide a separate, slower clock domain for peripheral interfaces.
To synchronize asynchronous signals between different clock domains.
To increase the overall operating frequency of the circuit.
To eliminate static leakage current in standby mode.

questions.question: In digital logic circuits, what property describes the maximum amount of unwanted input noise a gate can tolerate without causing an incorrect output logic level?
options: Noise Margin
Propagation Delay
Fan-out
Setup Time
Output Impedance

questions.question: What is the fundamental difference in how Static Random-Access Memory (SRAM) and Dynamic Random-Access Memory (DRAM) cells store a single bit of information?
options: SRAM stores a bit using a flip-flop, while DRAM stores it as a charge on a capacitor.
SRAM uses a resistor to store a bit, while DRAM uses a transistor.
DRAM is non-volatile, while SRAM is volatile.
SRAM requires periodic refreshing to retain data, while DRAM does not.
SRAM is primarily used for main system memory, while DRAM is used for CPU caches.

questions.question: In integrated circuit reliability, what phenomenon describes the gradual transport of metal atoms in an interconnect line under the influence of an electric current, potentially leading to opens or shorts over time and causing device failure?
options: Electromigration
Latch-up
Hot Carrier Injection
Dielectric Breakdown
Negative Bias Temperature Instability (NBTI)
